parsed_review.summary: CP00-716 is a descriptor-only RP23 External Integrations II finance-integrations P08 pack (risk B, 40 units, RP23.P08.M02.S15-RP23.P08.M04.S12) and its production_ready_flag (finance_integrations_p08_type_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor_verified) is fully supported by read-only inspection. Implementation (registry.js binding+requirements, service.js descriptor/case-set/packets, validators.js guards, index.js re-exports) matches the pack baseline exactly and reuses the established CP694-CP715 pattern, chaining from the CP715 descriptor with runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-717. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is all-false, every row is descriptor_only with runtime_execution=false and no raw/financial/credential payloads, permission-decision detail, audit body, or cross-tenant access; only boolean guard flags appear, no real data or secrets. Tests in model.test.js cover CP716 binding/coverage, descriptor runtime-closed, and contract projection. The contract sets current/latest/latest_projection to CP00-716 with top-level validation.valid=true and cp716 coverage+descriptor valid (errors=[]), generated deterministically by the validator script (control-plane artifact, not product runtime). command-evidence.json shows all required validation commands at exit 0. Manifest/adjudication/inspection correctly remain pending_review with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false (no premature claims). CP178+ hardening holds: readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, prompt 2481B under limit, no full diff/source embedded, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, summary-only prohibited for risk B. Authority boundary intact: Claude is read-only and non-final, human approval required for runtime opening, Hermes used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling and not embedded in product runtime. Unrelated dirty files (plan, UI control-room, workbook, LDIP) are correctly segregated with no scope contamination. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; the pack makes no enterprise-trust or runtime claim, so this CP closeout is not blocked.
